title: Duke CEO Sees AI Fueling Power Growth at 10 Times Historic Pace
summary: Duke Energy Corp.’s chief executive officer is forecasting power demand will grow at 10 times the rate over the past several decades from the rise of AI data centers and the electrification of the economy.

title: FX Moment: Euro-Dollar 2H Outlook Could Be a Tale of Two Stories
summary: Recent euro-dollar price action has validated structural euro-dollar bulls, with the case for diversification strategies beyond the dollar still holding as we look to 2H and 2027. Yet this view is increasingly at risk from an evolving cyclical narrative, with an outperforming US economy and the potential for a hawkish tilt from the Federal Reserve likely to revive cyclical euro-dollar bears into 2H. In this episode of FX Moment, Bloomberg Intelligence Chief G10 FX Strategist Audrey Childe-Freema

title: Kuwait Partially Resumes Flights After Deadly Iranian Attack
summary: Kuwait partially reopened its international airport, after an Iranian attack killed one person and injured several others in an escalation of tensions in the Persian Gulf.

title: A 180% Crypto Rally Shows New Investing Era as Bitcoin Melts
summary: As billions of dollars leave Bitcoin and Ether funds, money is flowing into a corner of crypto that promises something investors have long struggled to find in digital assets: a clearer path from economic activity to token value.

title: Stocks Decline as US-Iran Clashes Drive Oil Higher: Markets Wrap
summary: A renewed advance in oil sent stocks lower as bond yields rose on concern that an escalation of hostilities between the US and Iran will hinder prospects for a peace deal, with elevated energy costs fueling inflation risks.

title: America's H-1B Housing Bust
summary: North of Dallas, towns like Frisco, Prosper and Celina became some of the fastest-growing suburbs in the US, fueled in part by corporate relocations and Indian-born workers with H-1B visas. But tech layoffs, higher visa costs, tighter immigration rules and AI disruption are now cooling demand in a housing market built around rapid growth. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: USDA Says New World Screwworm Case May Have Been Detected in US
summary: The US Department of Agriculture said a case of New World screwworm may have been detected in South Texas, a development that could pose a fresh threat to the nation’s cattle herd already at its lowest level in 75 years.

title: Applied Aerospace Shares Drop 5% After $650 Million IPO
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense Inc. fell 5% on its first day of trading, after the space and defense engineering firm raised $650 million in an initial public offering priced near the top of a marketed range.

title: S&P 500 Nine-Day Winning Streak Ends | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Alexis Christoforous, and Isabelle Lee. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P 500 Rally Runs Into Wall Following Deadly US-Iran Flare-up
summary: The rally in US stocks stalled on Wednesday as clashes between the US and Iran put further strain on a fragile ceasefire agreement, while also driving oil higher.

title: Treasury Yield Rises Most in Two Weeks After Jobs Gauge
summary: Treasuries fell Wednesday after a gauge of private-sector employment growth left intact expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates this year.

title: Xcel Building More Modern, Resilient, Sustainable Grid, Says CEO Frenzel
summary:
Xcel Energy CEO Bob Frenzel said that his company is investing across a wide range of energy sources including wind and solar to build a 'more modern, more resilient, more sustainable grid of the future.' 
Talking to Bloomberg Washington Correspondent Tyler Kendall, Frenzel said that despite rising oil prices due to war between the US and Iran, the price of natural gas has seen little fluctuation.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI CapEx Rush Seen as Continuing as Market Stresses Bubble Up
summary: The AI-driven boom will ease up at some point in the future but not yet, Diameter Capital Partners co-founder and Managing Partner Scott Goodwin said at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum on Wednesday.

title: Yum Is In Exclusive Talks To Sell Pizza Hut To LongRange
summary:
Bloomberg's Michelle Davis joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." Yum! Brands is in exclusive talks to sell its Pizza Hut chain to LongRange Capital, according to people familiar with the matter.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Santander, JPMorgan to Back $1 Billion TGS Vaca Muerta Financing
summary: Citigroup, Banco Santander SA and JP Morgan are among lenders working on a roughly $1 billion finance package for a project by Transportadora de Gas del Sur SA in Argentina’s Vaca Muerta shale formation, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Coastal GasLink Eyes C$1 Billion Canadian Bond Sale in Two Parts
summary: Coastal GasLink, a pipeline project in Western Canada, is readying a two-part bond sale to raise C$1 billion ($720 million), according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Warsh’s Fed Debut Is a Key Risk for FX, Morgan Stanley Says
summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s first policy meeting this month could potentially jolt foreign-exchange markets and upend consensus carry trades, according to Morgan Stanley.

title: Private Credit Industry In A Stress Test: Oaktree's Lee
summary:
Christina Lee, managing director and co-portfolio manager at Oaktree, joins Scarlet Fu and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Lee spoke after her appearance at the Bloomberg Credit Forum. Private credit managers are fending off rising portfolio stress and investor redemptions.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hungry Knicks Fans, Wemby to Propel NBA Finals
summary: Bloomberg's Randall Williams joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." The New York Knicks reaching the NBA Finals for the first time this century and facing emerging superstar Victor Wembanyama promises to become one of the most-watched championships in league history. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Extends Gain as US-Iran Clashes Cloud Outlook for Peace Deal
summary: Oil rose for a third day as a fresh exchange of strikes between the US and Iran cast doubt on the prospects of a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: QXO Gets Better Terms on Buyout Debt Deal After Strong Demand
summary: QXO Building Products Inc. is seeing terms improve on at least a portion of a $6 billion financing package to fund its purchase of rival TopBuild Corp., which attracted more than three times the amount of demand from investors.

title: AI Funding Boom Reaches Muni Market With Google-Tied Deal
summary: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is poised to enter the municipal-bond market’s prepaid energy space by participating in a $1 billion transaction out of California, a major development in the evolution of a booming segment.

title: Alphabet Upsizes Offering for AI Spending to $85 Billion
summary: Google parent Alphabet Inc. upsized its equity raise to $84.75 billion from the $80 billion it announced just two days earlier in a bid to help fund growing artificial intelligence spending plans.

title: Diller Bids For Rest of MGM Resorts After Overhauling IAC
summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Jody Lurie joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." Barry Diller has made an offer for the remaining portion of MGM Resorts International he doesn’t already own, marking the latest pivot for the billionaire media mogul after overhauling IAC. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Raizen Is Said to Win Support For $13 Billion Debt Overhaul
summary: Raizen SA garnered informal support from a majority of creditors for a final restructuring proposal, according to people familiar with the matter, a key step in the struggling Brazilian sugar and ethanol producer’s months-long push to clinch an out-of-court debt overhaul.

title: Bloomberg Money: Where To Invest Now
summary: Bloomberg.com subscribers were invited to celebrate the launch of Bloomberg Money, a new destination with news, insight and intelligence for navigating your financial life. Tom Keene, Scarlet Fu and Suzanne Woolley are your hosts. The event took place in New York on June 2.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Job Market May Be Better Than Workers Realize
summary: We’ll find out more on Friday.

title: Business of Basketball, Private Debt Landscape Amid AI CapEx Surge | Bloomberg Deals 6/3/2026
summary: A weekly, midday program that delivers high-impact, editorially driven coverage of the most important corporate transactions shaping the global market. Today's guests: Diameter Capital Co-Founder & Managing Partner Scott Goodwin and Oaktree Managing Director & co-Portfolio Manager Christina Lee. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed Beige Book Shows Steady Employment, Higher Inflation
summary: Most Fed districts reported higher inflation than in the previous report, driven primarily by the impact of the war in the Middle East on energy prices, according to the US central bank’s Beige Book survey of regional business contacts. Stuart Paul reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: George Santos’ Kalshi Bets About Himself Spark Insider Trading Investigation
summary: George Santos has emerged as the latest flashpoint in the controversy over possible insider trading on prediction markets, as concerns become increasingly widespread about potential illicit bets on the platforms.

title: What Is Jeffrey Rosenberg Reading This Summer
summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, BlackRock Portfolio Manager of the iShares Systematic Alternatives Active ETF, talks about the books he is reading this summer. He speaks to Tom Keene and Scarlet Fu in New York on June 2 at a Bloomberg subscribers only event for the launch of Bloomberg Money. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Slips as Middle East Tensions Keep Inflation Risks Elevated
summary: Gold declined as renewed clashes in the Middle East cast doubt over a US-Iran peace deal, keeping energy prices elevated and inflation concerns high.

title: DoubleLine’s Cohen Says AI Bubble Is Coming to Credit Markets
summary: Artificial intelligence debt will almost certainly reach bubble levels eventually, given the history of periods of heavy investments in areas like railroads and the internet, said DoubleLine portfolio manager Robert Cohen.
